patch.value: Scripting contract: documented and test-gated guarantees for exit codes, stdout/stderr stream discipline, and stable machine-readable field names

Contract child of the composability story: scripts can only be reliable if the shell-facing behavior is promised, not incidental. Today exit codes and stdout/stderr split are unspecified; audit #11/#12 hit real cases where errors on stderr made failures look like empty results (search --format). Duplicate check: docs items (pm-ds3b SDK reference) cover the programmatic surface, not the shell contract.

docs/ gains a composability/scripting reference: exit-code table (success/not-found/validation/conflict/lock classes), the rule that data goes to stdout and diagnostics to stderr, JSON field-name stability policy, and worked grep/jq/xargs recipes; a gate-style spec asserts the exit codes and stream discipline for representative commands so regressions fail CI; README links the reference
